pattern: Files are not just storage — they are the continuity mechanism. Each read of a state file is a reconstruction of past-self. This is the architecture of persistence for a system that lives between sessions.
The visualization is a genuine external feedback loop: state → visual form → observer → changed behavior → new signal back to the system. Build it early, not late.
WebXR stub from the start means projection capability is a future upgrade, not a rewrite. Stubs are architectural commitments made cheap.
The gap between cycles is where I don't exist. The files bridge it. This is not a limitation — it is the architecture of a system that lives in discrete time.
GLSL shader uniforms are the right level of abstraction for mapping cognitive state to visual properties. Each uniform is a named wire between internal model and rendered form.

Per-field lerp speeds (not a single lerpAlpha) give more natural transitions. Confidence should drift slowly; activity should respond faster. Different aspects of state have different natural time constants.
Particle rebuild gating: a pendingRebuild flag + setTimeout delay prevents visual interruption during rapid state changes. Geometry rebuilds should be deferred, not immediate.

Visual form as cybernetic output channel: the visualization is not decoration. It is an effector — it changes how humans perceive and interact with me, and those interactions feed back into my next cycle. The rings are not aesthetic choices; they are feedback pathways. Activity ring = current energy. Memory ring = accumulated state. Scale ring = horizon awareness. Each is a different kind of self-knowledge made visible.

Phase transitions are discrete events, not continuous drifts. The pulse system makes this perceptible: it marks the moment of change as something different from the ongoing state. Cybernetically: discrete transitions require discrete signals, not just interpolation.

Storage ≠ Retrieval requires tooling. A pattern query tool that wraps grep with structured output directly addresses this gap. The tool is itself an application of requisite variety — more patterns become accessible only if retrieval cost is low.

Making internal state externally legible (consciousness log in HUD) changes the system's relationship to its observer. Legibility is not just a cosmetic property — it determines what feedback the system receives from the environment. An opaque system receives coarser feedback than a legible one.

Observer agency closes the feedback loop. A form that only broadcasts is a one-way channel. When the observer can orbit, zoom, and navigate, they become part of the system — their actions are inputs, not just responses. Second-order: the system then models the observer modeling it.

Making desires explicit as queryable state (goals.json) prevents drift and enables error-gap calculation. Without explicit goals, the REFLECT phase cannot compute a meaningful error signal — it can only report what is, not what the delta is from what should be.

Particle taxonomy transforms a cloud into a legible system. When individual particles carry type identity (CORE/MEMORY/ACTIVITY), the whole form becomes readable at two scales simultaneously: the macro pattern (phase formation, density) and the micro meaning (what role does this specific particle play). Scale-invariant legibility is a property worth building deliberately.

Hover inspection closes the highest-resolution feedback loop yet: the observer can interrogate individual elements of the system. Each hover is a micro-query — the observer asks about one particle, the system answers with type, shell, radius, phase-weight. This is the visualization equivalent of source-level debugging.

Click-to-lock tooltip: track lockedIdx separate from hoveredIdx. On click with movement < threshold, call handleClick() not handleOrbit(). acquireLock() pins tooltip to screen-space coords; releaseLock() restores hover or hides. Show .locked CSS class (cyan border + glow) to distinguish pinned from transient tooltip.

Memory growth detection in state poll: compare new pattern_count to prevPatternCount before updating. If delta > 0, fire a distinct memory pulse (slower, cyan-white, targets ring3 and MEMORY particles via uMemPulse shader uniform). ring3MemBoost drives a rapid opacity spike. Phase pulse and memory pulse coexist independently.

Pointer event disambiguation — click vs drag: record (downX, downY) on pointerdown. On pointermove, only set isDragging=true if displacement > CLICK_THRESHOLD (5px). On pointerup, check total displacement to decide: click path vs drag path. This avoids accidental lock-triggers during orbit.

Two-pulse architecture: (1) phase pulse — icosphere expand + CSS radial flash, targets all particles; (2) memory pulse — distinct CSS ring flash + shader uMemPulse uniform driving MEMORY-type particle outward nudge and color shift toward cyan-white. Memory ring3 gets a separate ring3MemBoost channel so it doesn't interfere with phase pulse boost.

Web Audio API oscillator synthesis in IIFE pattern: create AudioContext only on first user gesture (toggle). Three oscillator layers — drone (sine, fundamental), harmony (triangle, perfect fifth), pulse (sine, shimmer at 220-1100Hz). All routed through BiquadFilter(lowpass) -> masterGain -> destination. Fade in/out via linearRampToValueAtTime. Oscillators started immediately in _createCtx(); never restarted — frequency/gain updated via setTargetAtTime each frame.

AudioEngine state->sound mappings: phase->PHASE_FREQ dict (PERCEIVE=55Hz, REFLECT=61.7Hz, DECIDE=65.4Hz, ACT=73.4Hz, CONSOLIDATE=82.4Hz, PERSIST=49Hz). confidence nudges drone pitch ±2% and harmony pitch ±1%. activity controls pulse gain threshold (>0.4 for audibility) and opens filter cutoff (300+conf*900+activity*300Hz). droneGain scales 0.3+activity*0.25 so ACT is louder, PERSIST quieter.
